topic: uncertainty-aware pseudo-label selection for semi-supervised medical image classification under label noise

method_matrix.title: Pseudo-D: Informing Multi-View Uncertainty Estimation with Calibrated Neural Training Dynamics
method_matrix.method: The method records model predictions across training epochs for each sample, aggregates these predictions into pseudo-labels representing model uncertainty, calibrates these pseudo-labels using validation data through temperature scaling or Dirichlet calibration, and trains models on these uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels to enhance robustness.
method_matrix.best_result: Pseudo-D achieves best study-level AURC of 0.885 and MAE of 0.180

method_matrix.title: Sam-Driven Weakly Supervised Nodule Segmentation with Uncertainty-Aware Cross Teaching
method_matrix.method: The framework first generates three bounding box prompts (tight box, ellipse-based, and circle-based) from clinical annotations to guide SAM in producing pseudo-labels. It then selects optimal pseudo-labels using intersection/union operations and employs uncertainty-aware cross-teaching between two models trained on these labels.
datasets_used: Thyroid Ultrasound Dataset (422 left + 422 right images)
Breast Ultrasound Dataset (604 left + 151 right images)
method_matrix.best_result: Ours achieved DSC of 73.5±22.2 and HD95 of 32.8±71.5 on Thyroid Ultrasound, and DSC of 75.5±19.8 and HD95 of 52.3±36.5 on Breast Ultrasound

method_matrix.title: Addressing Data Scarcity in 3D Trauma Detection through Self-Supervised and Semi-Supervised Learning with Vertex Relative Position Encoding
method_matrix.method: The approach uses patch-based Masked Image Modeling to pre-train a 3D U-Net encoder on unlabeled CT volumes, then employs this encoder for downstream tasks including 3D injury detection with VDETR and 3D RPE, and multi-label injury classification.
datasets_used: RSNA 2023 dataset (3,147 patients, 3,206 CT series for classification)
206 labeled CT volumes with 3D bounding box annotations for 3D injury detection (144 train, 30 val, 32 test)
2,000 unlabeled CT volumes for semi-supervised learning
method_matrix.best_result: Semi-supervised learning achieved 56.57% mAP@0.50 (114% improvement) for 3D injury detection

method_matrix.title: Mutual Evidential Deep Learning for Medical Image Segmentation
method_matrix.method: MEDL employs two segmentation networks with different architectures to generate complementary predictions for unlabeled data, fuses them via class-aware evidential fusion, calculates reliability using Shannon entropy, and applies Fisher information-based uncertainty weighting to guide targeted learning.
method_matrix.best_result: Ours: ACDC RV DSC 89.01%, JAC 80.80%, ASD 7.28mm, 95HD 1.70mm; LV DSC 93.34%, JAC 87.81%, ASD 4.25mm, 95HD 1.23mm; Avg DSC 90.40%, JAC 82.94%, ASD 3.38mm, 95HD 0.83mm
limitations: The paper acknowledges that model performance can be diminished by biases in model recognition caused by low-quality pseudo-labels in semi-supervised medical segmentation co-learning frameworks

method_matrix.title: SemSim: Revisiting Weak-to-Strong Consistency from a Semantic Similarity Perspective for Semi-supervised Medical Image Segmentation
method_matrix.method: Semi-supervised learning (SSL) for medical image segmentation is a challenging yet highly practical task, which reduces reliance on large-scale labeled dataset by leveraging unlabeled samples. Among S
datasets_used: ACDC (200 cardiac MRI images from 100 patients)
baselines_compared: MT, UA-MT, EM, DCT, CCT, CPS, ICT, DAN, URPC, SSNet, ICT-Med, FixMatch, SCPNet, BCP-Net, CTCT, S4CVnet, MedFCT, CnT-B, DiffRect, CPC-SAM
method_matrix.best_result: SemSim achieved 0.896 DSC and 2.3mm 95HD on ACDC with 10% labeled data

method_matrix.title: Rethinking Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation: A Variance-Reduction Perspective
method_matrix.method: ARCO uses variance-reduced estimation with stratified group theory to improve contrastive learning for semi-supervised medical segmentation, incorporating specialized sampling techniques and multiple loss functions.
limitations: Focuses on medical image segmentation rather than classification
Framework designed specifically for contrastive learning scenarios
Does not explicitly address label noise challenges

method_matrix.title: EVIL: Evidential Inference Learning for Trustworthy Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation
method_matrix.method: Recently, uncertainty-aware methods have attracted increasing attention in semi-supervised medical image segmentation. However, current methods usually suffer from the drawback that it is difficult to
datasets_used: Automated Cardiac Diagnosis Challenge (ACDC) dataset (200 annotated short-axis cardiac MR-cine images from 100 patients)
method_matrix.best_result: EVIL achieves DSC of 89.43% with 30% labeled data
limitations: The paper mentions that current methods struggle to balance computational cost, estimation accuracy, and theoretical support in a unified framework.

method_matrix.title: Semi-supervised learning for medical image classification using imbalanced training data
method_matrix.method: ABCL creates a blended target distribution that combines OSP and ASP predictions with weighting determined by class frequencies, pulling both original and augmented predictions toward this target distribution using Kullback-Leibler divergence loss.
method_matrix.best_result: UDA-WeightedCE-ABCL achieved UAR of 0.68, G-mean of 0.66, and average AUC of 0.96 on HAM10000 dataset

method_matrix.title: Exploring Feature Representation Learning for Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation
method_matrix.method: The method employs a two-stage framework: first using boundary-aware contrastive loss on labeled data's boundary pixels to learn features, then applying prototype-aware contrastive loss on all pixels with pseudo labels. AUA estimates aleatoric uncertainty to adaptively weight consistency regularization between student and teacher models.
datasets_used: Colon tumor dataset (5 labeled, 95 unlabeled scans)
method_matrix.best_result: Ours achieves 79.81% Dice on Pancreas dataset (12 labeled, 48 unlabeled), surpassing state-of-the-art by 0.54%
method_matrix.compute_required: Training takes ~2x time vs single-stage methods; inference time similar to V-Net (4.70s vs 4.67s/sample)

method_matrix.title: Domain-Specific, Semi-Supervised Transfer Learning for Medical Imaging
method_matrix.method: MAKNet uses dense blocks with MAKConv layers, followed by standard conv, CBAM attention, and Mish activation. Semi-supervised training employs a teacher network (15 epochs) to generate pseudo-labels (>0.5 confidence) for unlabeled data, training a student network on concatenated labeled/unlabeled batches.
method_matrix.best_result: 0.9403 AUC on hand-labeled test set for LesaNet (TCIA) on DeepLesion dataset
limitations: Knowledge transfer across disparate domains is less effective

method_matrix.title: Learning Disentangled Stain and Structural Representations for Semi-Supervised Histopathology Segmentation
method_matrix.method: CSDS employs two specialized student networks (color and structure) trained on stain-augmented and structure-augmented inputs respectively, supervised by a shared teacher network updated via EMA, with uncertainty-weighted consistency losses.
method_matrix.best_result: CSDS achieves Dice of 85.06 ± 0.57 and Jaccard of 74.87 ± 0.82 on GlaS with 10% labeled data
limitations: Limited information available from the incomplete abstract

method_matrix.title: Uncertainty-Aware Extreme Point Tracing for Weakly Supervised Ultrasound Image Segmentation
method_matrix.method: The framework uses SAM2 with bounding box prompts derived from four extreme points to generate initial pseudo labels, which are progressively refined using an enhanced FGEPM algorithm with Monte Carlo dropout-based uncertainty estimation, while enforcing spatial consistency with USC loss and boundary alignment with box alignment loss.
datasets_used: UNS (Ultrasound Nerve Segmentation) dataset with 2,323 image-mask pairs
method_matrix.best_result: Our method achieved 67.70% IoU / 76.89% Dice on BUSI and 67.39% IoU / 79.12% Dice on UNS, surpassing fully supervised performance on BUSI
limitations: Limited to ultrasound images, may not generalize to other imaging modalities
Requires accurate identification of four extreme points for each structure
Uncertainty estimation relies on Monte Carlo dropout which may have limitations in capturing all sources of uncertainty

method_matrix.title: DRIVE: Dual-Robustness via Information Variability and Entropic Consistency in Source-Free Unsupervised Domain Adaptation
method_matrix.method: DRIVE employs a dual-model, two-stage approach with perturbation-induced robustness using Projected Gradient Descent (PGD) noise perturbations. Stage 1 aligns models on stable features using mutual information consistency loss, while Stage 2 dynamically adjusts perturbation levels based on Stage 1 losses.
limitations: Performance degradation on extremely low-resource target domains
Sensitivity to hyperparameters
Computational overhead of the proposed method
Limited evaluation on certain types of domain shifts

method_matrix.title: Anomaly Detection in Medical Imaging - A Mini Review
method_matrix.method: Qualitative literature review using Google Scholar and 4 search terms, filtering 120 papers to 45 relevant research items, then clustering by imaging modality and analyzing applications, methods, and motivations.
datasets_used: CT scans of skeleton or spines

method_matrix.title: Uncertainty-Guided Mutual Consistency Learning for Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation
method_matrix.method: A dual-task network with segmentation and signed distance regression branches, using Monte Carlo Dropout for uncertainty estimation and enforcing both intra-task (student-teacher) and cross-task (segmentation-regression) consistency losses.
method_matrix.best_result: UG-MCL achieved 90.16% Dice on LA dataset with 16 labeled scans and 64 unlabeled scans
limitations: The method is primarily designed for segmentation tasks and may not directly apply to classification tasks.

method_matrix.title: Reliable fairness auditing with semi-supervised inference
method_matrix.method: Combines small labeled dataset with large unlabeled dataset by imputing missing outcomes via regression with nonlinear basis functions, then evaluates fairness criteria on the completed dataset.
method_matrix.best_result: Infairness achieves lowest bias and variance for ∆TPR estimation on MIMIC-III
limitations: Limited to fairness auditing applications rather than general semi-supervised learning
May not address all types of fairness criteria comprehensively
Assumes availability of unlabeled data which might not always be accessible

method_matrix.title: Less Is More: Surgical Phase Recognition From Timestamp Supervision
method_matrix.method: Uses Monte Carlo Dropout for uncertainty estimation, diffuses timestamp labels to adjacent low-uncertainty frames, and employs loop training to optimize spatial and temporal extractors separately using only labeled frames.
limitations: The method is specifically designed for surgical videos where phases are long events, which might limit its applicability to other medical image classification tasks.

method_matrix.title: Complementing Semi-Supervised Learning with Uncertainty Quantification
method_matrix.method: The approach proposes an unsupervised uncertainty-aware objective that quantifies aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty to align distributions between labeled and unlabeled domains, complementing existing SSL techniques without requiring computationally expensive graph formation or similarity computations.
limitations: Existing SSL methods do not incorporate uncertainty of pseudo labels or unlabeled samples
Issues with noisy labels and out-of-distribution samples due to strong augmentations

method_matrix.title: Uncertainty-Aware Semi-Supervised Few Shot Segmentation
method_matrix.method: Meta-train a neural network to jointly predict segmentation and uncertainty, then refine pseudo labels using uncertainty estimates during inference to generate reliable prototypes from unlabeled images.
datasets_used: PASCAL-5i (20 classes, 4 folds, 5 classes per fold)
COCO-20i (80 classes, 4 folds, 20 classes per fold)
method_matrix.best_result: Ours + Iq + R achieves 56.27% mean-IoU (1-shot) and 64.73% (5-shot) on PASCAL-5i with M=12 unlabeled images

method_matrix.title: Uncertainty-Aware Distillation for Semi-Supervised Few-Shot Class-Incremental Learning
method_matrix.method: The framework operates in incremental sessions: (1) CE module uses class-balanced self-training to incorporate unlabeled data while preventing class imbalance; (2) UaD module performs uncertainty-aware distillation between reference and target models using exemplars; (3) exemplars are updated by filtering samples from current datasets. The loss function combines cross-entropy and distillation losses (Equation 4).
method_matrix.best_result: UaD-CE achieves 54.50% on CIFAR100, 50.52% on miniImageNet, and 60.72% on CUB200 in the final session
